assignment.title: Semantic Segmentation
assignment.subtitle: Deep Learning Project: ADE20K Semantic Segmentation with SegFormer & DeepLabV3+

dataset.name: ADE20K
dataset.keyFact: 150 classes with EXTREME frequency imbalance — top 2 classes (wall, floor) occupy >20% of all pixels; rarest classes <0.01%

architectures.name: SegFormer-B2
architectures.description: Hierarchical Vision Transformer with no positional encoding needed. Uses Efficient Self-Attention with sequence reduction. Global receptive field in all encoder stages.

architectures.name: DeepLabV3+ ResNet-101
architectures.description: Classic CNN with Atrous Spatial Pyramid Pooling (ASPP) for multi-scale context. Combines 5 parallel dilated convolutions (rates: 1, 6, 12, 18) plus global average pooling.
strengths: Excellent boundary detail via skip connections

architectures.name: DeepLabV3+ ConvNeXt-Base
architectures.description: Modernized CNN backbone incorporating Transformer design principles: 7×7 depthwise convolutions, LayerNorm, GELU activations, and inverted bottleneck blocks for stronger representation.

augmentations.name: Standard Albumentations
augmentations.description: Geometric (random crop, flip) + photometric (color jitter, CLAHE) transforms applied simultaneously to image and mask. Enforces invariance to lighting and scale changes.

augmentations.name: CutMix
augmentations.description: Cuts a random rectangular region from one image and pastes it into another. Forces the network to learn from partial objects in unconventional contexts.

augmentations.name: Copy-Paste
augmentations.description: Extracts objects via their ground-truth masks and artificially injects them into random scenes. Explicitly increases the frequency of rare minority classes.

augmentations.name: ClassMix
augmentations.description: Selects ~50% of semantic classes from a source image and blends them into a target image using natural object boundaries. Provides a highly realistic regularization signal.

augmentations.name: Optimized DataLoader
augmentations.description: Multi-process data loading with pin_memory and num_workers. Ensures the GPU is continuously fed with augmented batches without starvation bottlenecks.

lossFunctions.name: Focal Loss
lossFunctions.purpose: Down-weights easy examples and focuses training on hard, misclassified pixels — essential for extreme class imbalance

lossFunctions.name: Dice Loss
lossFunctions.purpose: Optimizes spatial overlap (region-level F1). Robust to class imbalance since it treats each class equally regardless of pixel count

lossFunctions.name: Boundary Loss
lossFunctions.purpose: Penalizes prediction errors near object boundaries using a distance map — produces sharper, more precise segmentation edges

lossFunctions.name: Lovász-Softmax
lossFunctions.purpose: Directly optimizes the per-class Jaccard index (IoU) via the Lovász extension of the discrete IoU loss — bonus component

trainingTechniques.name: Mixed Precision (AMP)
trainingTechniques.description: FP16 compute — halves VRAM usage and doubles training speed

trainingTechniques.name: Gradient Accumulation
trainingTechniques.description: Simulates a larger effective batch size without running out of GPU memory

trainingTechniques.name: Differential LRs
trainingTechniques.description: 0.1× LR for pretrained encoder / 1.0× LR for randomly initialized decoder

trainingTechniques.name: Model EMA
trainingTechniques.description: Exponential Moving Average of weights for a more stable validation ensemble

trainingTechniques.name: Warmup + PolyLR
trainingTechniques.description: Linear warmup then polynomial decay for smooth, stable convergence

trainingTechniques.name: Gradient Clipping
trainingTechniques.description: Caps gradient norms to prevent large loss spikes from corrupting weights

interpretability.shows: Which spatial regions each pixel attends to across MiT encoder stages — demonstrates global context aggregation
Regions with strongest learned features per encoder stage — shows the progression from low-level edges to high-level semantics
Encoder regions most critical for the dominant predicted class — highlights local, discriminative texture and edge features
Broader contextual activation regions — reflects the wider 7×7 depthwise convolution receptive field of ConvNeXt

reports.title: EDA Report
reports.subtitle: Problem Statement & ADE20K Dataset Analysis
items: ADE20K: 150 classes, 20,210 train / 2,000 validation images from MIT CSAIL
Class distribution analysis: top 2 classes (wall, floor) account for >20% of all pixels
Imbalance ratio ~10,000× — motivates use of Focal Loss + Dice Loss
Image size statistics and class count per image distribution
Comparative overview: SegFormer (Transformer) vs. DeepLabV3+ (CNN)

reports.title: Data Preparation Report
reports.subtitle: Dataset, DataLoader, and Augmentation Pipeline
items: ADE20KFileDataset: remaps class indices to 0–149, void pixels → 255 (ignore_index)
Standard augmentation via Albumentations: random crop, flip, color jitter, CLAHE
CutMix: pastes a random rectangular crop between two training images
Copy-Paste: copies objects by their ground-truth mask to boost rare class frequency
ClassMix: blends ~50% of semantic classes from a source image into a target image
Optimized DataLoader: pin_memory + multi-process workers for GPU data pipeline

reports.title: Methodology Report
reports.subtitle: Architecture, Training, Evaluation and Comparison of ≥2 Methods
items: Method 1: SegFormer-B2 — MiT encoder + All-MLP decoder (27.5M params)
Method 2: DeepLabV3+ ResNet-101 — ASPP + encoder-decoder (45.7M params)
Method 3: DeepLabV3+ ConvNeXt-Base — Modern CNN backbone (89.4M params)
Hybrid loss: Focal Loss + Dice Loss + Boundary Loss + Lovász-Softmax
Training: Mixed Precision (AMP), EMA, Gradient Accumulation, Differential LRs
Metrics: mIoU, Mean Dice Score, Pixel Accuracy, Per-class IoU (150 classes)

reports.title: Experimental Results
reports.subtitle: Quantitative Tables, Training Curves, Analysis, and Discussion
items: Model comparison table: mIoU, Dice Score, Pixel Accuracy, FPS, and parameter count
ConvNeXt-Base achieves the highest mIoU (47.94%) at the cost of higher memory usage
SegFormer-B2 delivers the best accuracy-to-efficiency ratio across all models
Per-class IoU: wall/floor score high; microwave/clock score low (long-tail challenge)
Training curves: loss and mIoU on validation set across epochs
Confusion matrix (top-20 classes) and failure mode analysis

reports.title: Extension Reports
reports.subtitle: Interpretability (Attention/Grad-CAM) and Benchmarking (FPS/FLOPs/Memory)
items: SegFormer Attention Maps: global context aggregation across MiT encoder stages
DeepLabV3+ Grad-CAM: local vs. broad receptive field comparison (ResNet vs. ConvNeXt)
FPS Benchmarking: GPU warm-up (10 iterations) + 100-iteration timing with CUDA sync
FLOPs & parameter counting: SegFormer-B2 is most efficient on FLOPs/mIoU ratio
Pareto Analysis: FPS vs. mIoU scatter plot to identify optimal deployment model

methodologyComparison.description: Comparison of 3 semantic segmentation methods on ADE20K validation set (150 classes):
